title: Mouse debug trigger ignores normal top-edge clicks
reasoning: The nullish-coalescing expression is parsed as clientY ?? (0 > 80), not (clientY ?? 0) > 80. For normal MouseEvent values, the if condition receives the numeric clientY directly, so any click with clientY greater than 0 is truthy and returns early. As a result, the documented-style five-click trigger only works at exactly y=0 instead of anywhere in the top 80 pixels, while the touch path has the intended comparison.
reproduction: Click five times near the top of the page at clientY=10 without ?debug=1; the panel remains hidden because each click returns before recording the tap.
recommendation: Store clientY in a local variable and compare it explicitly, for example `const y = e.clientY ?? 0; if (y > 80) return; ... push({ time: now, y });`.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are included for DebugPanel or the hidden gesture behavior.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a component/unit test that dispatches five click events with clientY values between 1 and 80 and asserts that the DebugPanel becomes visible; also assert clientY > 80 does not trigger it.
minimumFixScope: Update the onClick handler in frontend/src/components/DebugPanel.tsx and add focused coverage for the click gesture.
